response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development with a strong background in C and C++ (e.g., National Instruments) but lacks recent and deep experience in C++ for industrial automation or security-critical systems. Their expertise is heavily skewed toward web/mobile development (JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js) and other languages (D, Java), with no visible focus on industrial protocols, cloud platforms (Azure), or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices. The resume does not highlight message brokers, multi-threading, or cybersecurity standards like IEC 62443.
strengths: Strong background in C and C++ (e.g., National Instruments)
Experience with low-latency and high-performance systems (e.g., micro-services at Dunnhumby)
Proven track record in software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, refactoring)
concerns: Lacks recent and relevant C++ experience for industrial automation or cloud solutions
No evidence of exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA) or IEC 62443
Minimal focus on secure coding practices or SDL in the described roles
